message:
fix(robot): accept trailing-slash directory prefixes in matchesPattern

Fix matchesPattern to recognize patterns ending with "/" as directory
prefixes. Previously, "dir/" would not match "dir/file.go" because
the function only checked strings.HasPrefix(filePath, pattern+"/"),
and appending "/" to "dir/" produced "dir//" which never matched.

Now, if the pattern already ends with "/", it is used directly as
the prefix without appending another slash. This aligns with how
users naturally express directory patterns in .gitignore-style
configuration.

Add three test cases covering trailing-slash patterns: exact match,
nested subdirectory match, and non-match.

Resolves bd-3nhcd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -284,6 +284,9 @@ func TestMatchesPattern(t *testing.T) {
 		{"dir/file.go", "dir", true},
 		{"dir/sub/file.go", "dir", true},
 		{"other/file.go", "dir", false},
+		{"dir/file.go", "dir/", true},
+		{"dir/sub/file.go", "dir/", true},
+		{"other/file.go", "dir/", false},
 
 		// Glob patterns
 		{"file.go", "*.go", true},
